level_signals: Owns quote-to-close process and commercial governance workflows independently
Comp range $125,000–$180,000 consistent with mid-to-senior IC in RevOps
Expected to build and scale operational infrastructure, not just execute tasks

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in Deal Desk, Sales Operations, or commercial operations within SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, or infrastructure companies
Strong understanding of enterprise pricing structures, usage based pricing, and contract governance
Experience administering CPQ systems such as Salesforce CPQ, DealHub, or Conga
Strong CRM proficiency with Salesforce preferred
Understanding of revenue recognition principles and enterprise contract structures
Experience partnering cross functionally with Sales, Finance, and Legal teams
Experience managing multiple complex deals simultaneously
Active use of AI tools for workflow automation or commercial operations
Successful completion of a background check

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in cloud infrastructure, AI infrastructure, developer tools, or ML platforms
Experience with usage based pricing models and savings plan structures
Experience implementing or migrating CPQ systems
Experience working with CLM platforms such as Ironclad, Juro, or DocuSign CLM
Experience supporting enterprise GTM organizations in high growth environments

explicitly_excluded: Non-US work authorization / visa sponsorship: 'we are currently unable to sponsor employment visas'

scope.surface: Quote-to-close process and commercial governance for RunPod's enterprise sales motion
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS/IaaS, usage-based pricing, enterprise infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Contract repository completeness and CRM alignment
core_responsibilities: Manage the review and approval process for non standard enterprise deals
Support custom pricing structures, savings plans, multi year agreements, and volume discounts
Establish and maintain Deal Desk SLAs with the Sales organization
Own CPQ implementation and administration in partnership with Revenue Operations and Finance
Maintain pricing rules, discount matrices, approval workflows, and product catalogs
Maintain pricing and discount governance standards
Partner with Finance on bookings definitions and revenue recognition requirements
Partner with Legal on contract review workflows and approval paths
Manage executed contract repositories and ensure CRM alignment
Build operational systems supporting renewals, amendments, and expansion workflows
Ensure deal structures and contract values are accurately reflected in CRM systems
Leverage AI tools for contract review, workflow automation, pricing analysis, and approval routing

green_flags: Equity (stock options) offered to all team members
Strong company growth signal: $120M ARR, 500K+ developers, founded 2022
Clear ownership scope and cross-functional partners defined in JD
AI-powered operations emphasis signals forward-thinking team
red_flags: No visa sponsorship available — explicitly excludes international candidates needing sponsorship
No minimum years of experience specified, making seniority expectations ambiguous
Salary range is wide ($55K spread) — 'will be narrowed during the interview process', suggesting significant variance based on location and experience
CPQ Salesforce is listed as 'preferred' in requirements but also as a must-have requirement — mild inconsistency in JD language

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of ownership responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not explicitly listed)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'We welcome every qualified candidate eligible to work in the United States' and 'remote work first' language)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'narrowed based on... location' language)
ambiguities: Salesforce CRM is listed as 'preferred' within a requirements bullet ('Strong CRM proficiency with Salesforce preferred') but appears in the Requirements section — treated as hard here with a note that Salesforce specifically is preferred while general CRM proficiency is required.
The JD says 'Most roles are remote work first' — not a guarantee this specific role is remote; treated as remote-US given the US work authorization requirement and no office mentioned.
No explicit years of experience required — seniority level inferred from comp and responsibility scope only.
